description_long: This object is a robust, rectangular guard stone constructed from stacked, roughly hewn granite blocks, forming a solid pillar. The front face features a prominent metal plaque, likely for commemorative or informational purposes, and is adorned with a decorative stone relief at the top, adding a touch of ornamentation. The stone blocks are irregular in texture and coloration, with visible mortar lines and some weathering, suggesting long-term outdoor exposure. The base is surrounded by a patch of earth and gravel, indicating it is typically installed at ground level. The sides and rear are plain, with no additional markings or features, emphasizing its utilitarian function as a protective barrier. The overall appearance is sturdy and imposing, designed to withstand impacts and demarcate boundaries in urban or historical settings.
description: A sturdy, rectangular granite guard stone with a metal plaque and decorative relief on the front, set on a gravelly base. Its weathered stone blocks and solid construction make it ideal for use as a protective urban barrier.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stacked granite blocks and the rough, weathered texture, with no visible plaque or ornamentation.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the metal plaque, decorative stone relief at the top, and the most detailed features of the guard stone.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the stone block structure and some discoloration from weathering.
description_view_3: Rear view with plain stone blocks, lacking any plaques or decorative elements, highlighting the utilitarian aspect.
attribute_location: Typically found at the entrance to driveways, along urban street corners, or at the perimeter of historical sites, often positioned to protect buildings or pedestrian areas from vehicle encroachment.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, standing upright and firmly anchored to the ground or partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible weathering and discoloration on the stone surfaces, especially near the base due to contact with soil and moisture. Some chipping and smoothing on edges from occasional vehicle or pedestrian contact, and possible patina or corrosion on the metal plaque from exposure to the elements.
attribute_historical_markings: A metal plaque with engraved or embossed text is present on the front face, along with a decorative stone relief at the top. No other inscriptions or symbols are visible.
